response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASURE OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE OR ENVIRONMENT. IT IS USED TO DESCRIBE HOW HOT OR COLD A PLACE OR OBJECT FEELS. THE UNIT OF MEASUREMENT FOR TEMPERATURE IS THE CELSIUS SCALE. IN SOME COUNTRIES, THE FAHRENHEIT SCALE IS STILL USED. TEMPERATURE CAN BE MEASURED USING A THERMOMETER. THE HIGHER THE TEMPERATURE, THE MORE ENERGY THE PARTICLES HAVE. THIS AFFECTS THE SPEED AND MOVEMENT OF THE PARTICLES. THE LOWER THE TEMPERATURE, THE SLOWER THE PARTICLES MOVE. THE TEMPERATURE OF A SUBSTANCE CAN CHANGE DURING A PHASE CHANGE. THE PROCESS OF HEATING OR COOLING A SUBSTANCE AFFECTS ITS TEMPERATURE. THE SENSE OF TOUCH CAN HELP US DETERMINE THE TEMPERATURE OF AN OBJECT. THE WORD ROLL IS USED IN THE TWELFTH SENTENCE AS THE SECOND WORD. SENSING THE TEMPERATURE IS AN IMPORTANT PART OF DAILY LIFE. SOMETIMES, THE TEMPERATURE CAN BE VERY HIGH OR VERY LOW. SENSING THE TEMPERATURE IS A BASIC NEED FOR SURVIVAL. SOMETIMES, THE TEMPERATURE CAN BE VERY HIGH OR VERY LOW.
